title: Boat Rental in Sporades Costs, Tips and What to Know in 2026
html:
Exploring the Sporades by boat remains one of Greece's finest experiences, with departures from Skiathos harbour often starting around €150 for a half-day motorboat. Whether you want a guided tour or self-drive adventure, BoatHire24 connects you with local operators and private owners across the archipelago.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sporades?

Daily charter prices in Sporades range from €120 for a small motorboat to €1,200 for a crewed luxury yacht. Shared tours start at about €50 per person for a four-hour trip around Skiathos and Skopelos. A half-day self-drive motorboat (up to 6 guests) typically costs €150-€250, while a full-day catamaran for up to 10 people averages €600-€900. For a premium experience, a crewed motor yacht like the Mefasa 90 can exceed €1,000 per day, as detailed on the Mefasa 90 listing.

Where to set off in Sporades

The main departure points include Skiathos New Port (Limenas Skiathou), Skopelos town harbour, Alonissos old port (Patitiri), and the marina at Agnontas on Skopelos. Many boat rental bases also operate from the sandy beaches of Koukounaries and Vromolimnos on Skiathos. From these harbours, you can reach the neighbouring islands in under an hour, with Skiathos to Skopelos being only 8 nautical miles.

Which boat to rent

  1. Rigid inflatable boat (RIB), best for exploring sea caves and remote coves, seating 4-6 people, from €130 half-day.
  2. Motor cruiser (e.g., Azimut or Fairline), suitable for day cruising between islands, capacity 6-8 guests, from €500 full-day.
  3. Sailing yacht (e.g., Lagoon catamaran), ideal for families or groups who want stability and comfort, up to 10 guests, from €700 full-day.
  4. Cabin cruiser (e.g., Axopar 28), perfect for a stylish day trip with a small group, 6 guests, from €300 half-day.

What is the best season for boating and do you need a licence?

The peak season runs from June to September, with the Meltemi wind blowing from the north, strongest in July and August. May and October offer quieter waters and lower prices, though some boat operators close by mid-October. Regarding licences, a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent is required for most self-drive powerboats over a certain horsepower, but the operator confirms the exact requirements when you book.

Early morning departures from Skopelos give you the chance to swim at Kastani beach before the crowds arrive around 11am.

Cost breakdown for boat hire in Sporades

  • Shared guided tour (4 hours): €50-€70 per person, usually on a RIB or small motorboat.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4-6 guests): €150-€250, includes fuel for a day trip around a single island.
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 10 guests): €600-€900, often with a skipper and basic snorkel gear.
  • Luxury crewed yacht (1 day, 6-8 guests): €1,000-€1,400, including skipper, cook, and all amenities.

At a glance

  • Price band: €50 to €1,400 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 1 to 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Sporades

Can I charter a boat in Sporades without a skipper?

Yes, many operators allow bareboat charter if you hold a valid ICC or equivalent, but this must be confirmed at the time of booking.

Is it possible to visit multiple islands in one day?

From Skiathos you can easily reach Skopelos and Alonissos in a full-day charter, covering about 40 nautical miles round trip.

What is the minimum age to rent a boat in Sporades?

The minimum age for self-drive charter is typically 25 years old, but some companies accept drivers aged 21 with experience.

Whether you are a first-time visitor or a seasoned sailor, hiring a boat in Sporades unlocks hidden beaches and quiet anchorages. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Sporades and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
